name: Set Slot
desc: Sets a single slot in an inventory window. Used for cursor updates, specific slot changes, and armor slot updates.

fields.name: item
fields.desc: ItemStack to place in slot (null = clear)

notes: windowId=-1 special case: updates the cursor item (the item held by the mouse cursor). windowId=0: updates player inventory. S2F is sent for EVERY slot change — armor equips, hotbar changes, cursor picks up/drops. Inventory modules track S2F to detect armor swaps and item pickups.

implementation.overview: S2FPacketSetSlot updates a single inventory slot. Three fields: windowId (-1=cursor, 0=player inv), slot index, and the ItemStack (null = clear). Used for cursor item tracking, armor swaps (slots 5-8), and hotbar updates. InventoryManager modules read S2F to detect when armor is equipped (slot 5=helmet through 8=boots), and AutoPot detects when potion items change in the hotbar. Disabler modules may cancel S2F to prevent the client from seeing inventory changes, causing inventory desync.

modules.name: Inventory Manager / AutoArmor Tracking
modules.purpose: Track inventory slot changes via S2FPacketSetSlot to detect item pickups, armor swaps, and cursor changes without polling. AutoArmor uses S2F to detect when new armor items enter the inventory.
modules.how_it_works: On each S2F, read windowId, slot, and item. If windowId=0 (player inventory) and slot is an armor slot (5-8), the module checks if the new item is better than currently equipped. If windowId=-1, the cursor item changed — used to detect when items are being dragged. The module tracks these changes to trigger actions without waiting for the next tick update.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleSetSlot(S2FPacketSetSlot) — updates the cursor stack (windowId=-1) or a container slot.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Cancelling S2F causes the client inventory to desync from server — items may appear duplicated or missing. Disabler modules that cancel S2F use it to hide inventory modifications from anti-cheats.

modules.name: Disabler (S2F Cancel)
modules.purpose: Cancel S2FPacketSetSlot to prevent the client from seeing specific inventory changes. Used to hide item manipulation from anti-cheat trackers.
modules.how_it_works: When the Disabler is in a mode that manipulates inventory, S2F packets for specific slots are cancelled. The client never sees the slot update, so it maintains its previous (possibly fake) inventory state.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Cancelling S2F for specific slots causes local inventory desync. The player may see items in slots that the server thinks are empty.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleSetSlot(S2FPacketSetSlot) — updates single slot or cursor. Inventory modules track this for item change detection. Disabler modules cancel it.

Server: Sent by the server whenever a single inventory slot changes — item pickup, armor equip, cursor grab, enchantment result, anvil rename, creative tab drag.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: Inventory component tracks S2F for armor/hotbar changes.
Sigma 4.11: InventoryManager reads S2F for slot tracking. AutoArmor detects armor S2F.
Gugustus: Disabler cancels S2F for slot hiding.
Tenacity 5.1: Disabler may cancel S2F in certain modes.
RektSky B5: No specific S2F module.
Jigsaw 0.26: Vanilla handler only.
